questions.explanation: Any positive number is greater than any negative number, so 4 > -3. A common mistake is focusing on the minus sign as if it makes the whole comparison larger.
Among negative numbers, the number closer to zero is greater. Since -2 is closer to zero than -9, -2 > -9. A common mistake is thinking the larger digit makes the number smaller.
Zero is greater than any negative number, so 0 > -1. A common mistake is forgetting that zero sits to the right of all negative numbers on the number line.
-1 is greater than -6 because it is closer to zero. A common mistake is choosing -6 because 6 is a larger digit.

questions.questionText: Choose the correct comparison for 7 and 0.
questions.explanation: Any positive number is greater than zero, so 7 > 0. A common mistake is thinking zero is automatically greater because it has no sign.
-10 is farther left on the number line than -3, so -10 < -3. A common mistake is thinking 10 being a larger digit makes -10 greater.
A warmer temperature is represented by the greater number. Since 2 > -4, 2 degrees is warmer. A common mistake is thinking negative numbers are warmer because the digit looks larger.

Among negative numbers, the one closer to zero is greater. Since -5 is closer to zero than -8, -5 > -8. A common mistake is comparing the digits without thinking about their locations on the number line.
Any negative number is less than any positive number, so -12 < 5. A common mistake is seeing 12 as a large digit and forgetting the negative sign.
Negative numbers are less than zero and positive numbers. That makes -2 the smallest number in the set. A common mistake is picking 0 because it looks neutral.

questions.questionText: A student says -9 > -4 because 9 is greater than 4. What is the mistake?
options: The student forgot that farther left means smaller
The student should always choose the larger digit
questions.correctAnswer: The student forgot that farther left means smaller
questions.explanation: On the number line, -9 is farther left than -4, so it is smaller, not greater. A common mistake is comparing the digits and forgetting how negative numbers work.

questions.questionText: Which number belongs in the blank: -3 ___ -3?
questions.explanation: A number is always equal to itself, so -3 = -3. A common mistake is thinking the negative sign changes one side more than the other.
-1 is to the left of 0 on the number line, so -1 < 0. A common mistake is thinking zero is less because it has no sign.

Among negative numbers, the value closer to zero is greater. Since -11 is closer to zero than -15 or -20, it is the greatest of the three. A common mistake is choosing -20 because 20 is the largest digit.

questions.questionText: An elevator is at floor -2 and then moves to floor 3. Which floor is greater?
questions.explanation: Positive floors are greater than negative floors, so floor 3 is greater than floor -2. A common mistake is thinking underground floors count as larger because the digit 2 is smaller than 3.

questions.questionText: Which number line idea correctly compares -7 and -2?
options: They are at the same point
Both are to the right of zero
questions.explanation: Numbers farther to the right are greater. Since -2 is to the right of -7, -2 > -7. A common mistake is thinking -7 is greater because 7 is a larger digit.
